focusAreas.name: Implementation Completeness
questions: Did the change implement real behavior instead of mocks, placeholders, pass stubs, or hardcoded success?
Are there TODO/FIXME comments or temporary shortcuts left in production paths?
Does the implementation preserve existing public contracts and avoid fake-green tests?

focusAreas.name: Hledac Architecture Invariants
questions: Does the change preserve uv-managed Python 3.14 and avoid global pip or pyenv assumptions?
Does it respect M1 8GB constraints: bounded memory, bounded concurrency, no unnecessary heavy dependencies?
Does async code avoid lifecycle leaks, unclosed sessions, unsafe run_until_complete, and broken cancellation semantics?

focusAreas.name: Integration and Ownership
questions: Is the code wired into the canonical owner instead of adding a duplicate parallel path?
Are teardown, fail-soft behavior, and observability handled where this project expects them?
Are tests or probes aligned with the current architecture rather than old legacy surfaces?
